title: Small Teams vs. Big Teams: Productivity & Meeting Madness! #shorts
summary: Small agile teams are portrayed as more productive and efficient than large teams, which are characterized as bogged down in excessive meetings. The argument suggests that efficiency correlates with team size.
writeup: This short from Cyril Imhof contrasts small and large teams on productivity. It argues that small agile teams move faster and operate more efficiently, while large teams get bogged down in excessive meetings. The central claim is that efficiency correlates with team size, favoring smaller groups.
